records.ety: [AS. crāwan; akin to D. kraijen, G. krähen, cf. Lith. groti to croak. √24. Cf. Crake.]
records.morph: [imp. Crew (kr) or Crowed (krōd); p. p. Crowed (Crown (krōn), Obs.); p. pr. & vb. n. Crowing.]

defs: To make the shrill sound characteristic of a cock, either in joy, gayety, or defiance.
To shout in exultation or defiance; to brag.
To utter a sound expressive of joy or pleasure.
quotes: The sweetest little maid, That ever crowed for kisses.

records.ety: [AS. crāwe a crow (in sense 1); akin to D. kraai, G. krähe; cf. Icel. krāka crow. So named from its cry, from AS. crāwan to crow. See Crow, v. i. ]

defs: A bird, usually black, of the genus Corvus, having a strong conical beak, with projecting bristles. It has a harsh, croaking note. See Caw.
notes: ☞ The common crow of Europe, or carrion crow, is Corvus corone. The common American crow is Corvus Americanus. See Carrion crow, and Illustr., under Carrion.

defs: A bar of iron with a beak, crook, or claw; a bar of iron used as a lever; a crowbar.
quotes: Get me an iron crow, and bring it straight Unto my cell.

defs: The cry of the cock. See Crow, v. i., 1.
The mesentery of a beast; -- so called by butchers.
